content: Preventive care encompasses three levels: (1) Primary prevention - preventing disease onset (vaccination, smoking cessation, healthy diet); (2) Secondary prevention - early detection before symptoms (cancer screening, blood pressure monitoring, lipid screening); (3) Tertiary prevention - preventing complications in established disease (diabetes foot care, cardiac rehabilitation). USPSTF grades recommendations A (strongly recommended) through D (recommended against), with I for insufficient evidence. Cost-effectiveness varies widely: childhood vaccination ($5-50/QALY), colorectal cancer screening ($5,000-20,000/QALY), while some preventive interventions exceed treatment cost-effectiveness.
